Notice Of Motion And Defendant-Cross-Complainant 1979 Union Street Corporation Dba The Blue Lights Motion For Protective Order To Compel Continued Independent Medical Examination With Specified Conditions; Request For Sanctions Against Plaintiff Aaron Abel And His Attorney Of Record Joseph May **Discovery Motion Set At 9:30**
Off Calendar May-24-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Joseph May of Law Office of Joseph S. May telephone number 415-781-3333 for Plaintiff Aaron Abel.
Mark Mittelman of Law Offices of Mark R. Mittelman telephone number 925-256-0677 for Defendant 1979 Union Street Corporation.

The matter was argued. The parties came to an agreement with respect to limitations on question to be asked at the medical examation (stated on the record). The tentative ruling is confirmed. DEFENDANT 1979 UNION STREET CORPORATION'S Motion For Protective Order To Compel Continued Independent Medical Examination With Specified Conditions is granted and its request for monetary sanctions is denied. Defendant did not waive its right to seek the relief it seeks by this motion by failing to file a motion prior to the date plaintiff Aaron Abel appeared at Dr. Lundy's office, although in retrospect defendant should have raised and sought resolution of this dispute before that date. There is no authority supporting Mr. Abel's waiver argument and it was reasonable for defendant's counsel to assume that, notwithstanding the objections asserted to the notice of the examination, Dr. Lundy would be able to conduct an examination that included the asking of relevant medical history questions. Mr. May's actions at Dr. Lundy's office constituted an impermissible disruption of Dr. Lundy's efforts to conduct an examination of Mr. Abel. A physical examination of a party necessarily entails asking relevant medical history questions, even if the answers to such questions could be found in other materials such as a deposition transcript or medical records. Mr. Abel must make himself available on a date convenient to Dr. Lundy within the next three weeks for a medical examination and at that examination Dr. Lundy is permitted to ask Mr. Abel questions about Mr. Abel's medical history relevant to the injuries claimed by Mr. Abel. Because the issue raised by this motion should have been dealt with prior to the date Mr. Abel appeared at Dr. Lundy's office, there was substantial justification for Mr. Abel's opposition to the motion on the grounds of waiver and thus monetary sanctions are denied. Mr. Mittelman is prepare a form of order that reflects what was stated and provide it to Mr. May for review.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
